content: sd block/unblock commands: block.ts uses --by flag; unblock.ts uses --from (specific) or --all (closed blockers). Both maintain bidirectional blocks/blockedBy arrays atomically under lock. Import Command with import type for biome compliance.

sd label command: label.ts uses dep.ts subcommand pattern (add/remove/list/list-all). Labels are free-form strings, normalized lowercase/trimmed on write. Stored as labels?: string[] on Issue — empty arrays become undefined (same as blocks/blockedBy). sd create uses --labels (comma-separated), sd update uses --add-label/--remove-label/--set-labels (comma-separated, applied in order: set→add→remove). sd list filters: --label (AND), --label-any (OR), --unlabeled.

name: sd-list-ready-default-sort
description: sd list and sd ready default to priority asc (P0 first), tie-break by createdAt desc. Override via --sort priority|created|updated|id. Sort logic lives in src/sort.ts (sortIssues, isSortMode, VALID_SORT_MODES) and is shared by both commands.

name: global-format-flag
description: Output-emitting commands (list/ready/show/blocked/stats) accept --format <markdown|compact|plain|ids|json>. --json is preserved as alias for --format json. resolveFormat() in src/format.ts parses both flags; ids mode emits issue IDs one per line for shell pipelines (sd list --format ids | xargs sd close). plain mode strips ANSI via stripAnsi() helper.

content: ANSI-stripping in plain format mode: use stripAnsi() in src/format.ts which builds the regex via String.fromCharCode(27) + new RegExp() to satisfy biome's noControlCharactersInRegex lint rule (a literal /\x1b.../ regex fails lint).

name: shared-issue-filter-helper
description: src/filter.ts hosts applyIssueFilters + filterOptionsFromFlags consumed by both sd list and sd ready. Filters: type, assignee, label (AND), label-any (OR), unlabeled. Add new shared filters here so both commands stay in sync.

name: find-then-narrow-no-bang
description: Replace 'arr[idx]!' after findIndex with the narrow-then-check pattern: 'const issue = issues[idx]; if (!issue) throw...'. TS narrows issue to T after the check, so no non-null assertion needed. Applied across block/close/dep/label/tpl/unblock/update commands.
